textPlain: High availability is touted as a reason for their high prices, but I swear I read about major cloud outages far more than I experience any outages at Hetzner.
For me the same. It's very confusing that status page [1] is green[1]: https://azure.status.microsoft/en-us/status
For us, it looks like most services are still working (eastus and eastus2). Our AKS cluster is still running and taking requests. Failures seem limited to management portal.
Github Actions and Codespaces degraded.

On our end, our VMs are still working, so our gitlab instance is still up. Our services using Azure App Services are available through their provided url. However, Front Door is failing to resolve any domains that it was responsible for.
FYI: https://status.cloud.microsoft/
Azure portal still insists the issue is jsut with Console.We had to bypass the Frontdoor
We're on Office 365 and so far it's still responding. At least Outlook and Teams is.
>Last week AWS, now this.This is not the first or second time this happened, multiple Hyperscaler failed one by one.
We saw all incoming traffic to our app drop to zero at about 15:45. I wonder how long this one will take to fix.
Oh, well, I'm sure Azure will be given the same pass that AWS got here recently when they had their 12-hour outage...
Not seeing it. I have VMs in US East and Netherlands and they're up.
We're quickly learning who's relying on a single cloud provider.
things seem to be coming back up now
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45748661
Yup, see it as well.
now aws down again?
Ostensible reason.The real reason is that outages are not your fault. Its the new version of "nobody ever got fired for buying IBM" - later it became MS, and now its any big cloud provider.
Same with DigitalOcean. I run one box and it hasnt gone down for like 2 years
It's just the admin portal.

That status page is never red. Absolutely useless.> There are currently no active events. Use Azure Service Health to view other issues that may be impacting your services.Links to a page on Azure Portal which is down...

They added a message at the same time as your comment:"We are investigating an issue with the Azure Portal where customers may be experiencing issues accessing the portal. More information will be provided shortly."
Which itself is^H^H was down.  Wow.
They don't run on Azure!
Same exact time for us as well.
I didn't realize AWS got a pass?
